# Theorem: Repeated Co-Action Becomes Unlikely Under Independence

## Statement
Let two independent accounts each act on objects from a large set. If probability of matching one object within time window \(\Delta\) is \(p\), then probability of at least \(r\) matches over \(n\) opportunities is:

\[
P(X\ge r)=\sum_{k=r}^{n}\binom{n}{k}p^k(1-p)^{n-k}
\]

For small \(p\), this probability declines rapidly as \(r\) grows.

## Relevance
One coincidental match means little. Repeated near-synchronous matches can become statistically suspicious.
